title: Agentic AI Business Solutions Architect (AB-100)
subtitle: Design Multi-Agent AI Architectures for the Enterprise
description: Demonstrate expertise in designing AI-first enterprise architectures that orchestrate multi-agent solutions with Microsoft Copilot, Foundry, and Dynamics 365
overview: This expert-level course prepares you for the AB-100 certification exam. You will learn to design and lead AI-first enterprise architectures, orchestrate multi-agent AI solutions, and integrate Microsoft Copilot, Azure AI Foundry, and Dynamics 365 to drive business transformation. This is the most advanced certification in the AB series and requires an active Associate-level Microsoft certification as a prerequisite.

learningObjectives.title: AI-First Enterprise Architecture
learningObjectives.description: Design enterprise architectures where AI agents are first-class components, not afterthoughts

learningObjectives.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration
learningObjectives.description: Orchestrate multiple AI agents to collaborate on complex business processes

learningObjectives.title: Platform Integration
learningObjectives.description: Integrate Microsoft Copilot, Azure AI Foundry, and Dynamics 365 into one connected solution

learningObjectives.title: Governance at Scale
learningObjectives.description: Implement governance, monitoring, and lifecycle management for AI agents in production

modules.title: AI-First Architecture Patterns
modules.description: Design patterns for building enterprise systems around AI capabilities
topics: AI-first vs. AI-augmented architecture trade-offs
Reference architectures for agentic AI systems
Data architecture for AI agents: retrieval, grounding, memory
Security architecture for autonomous agents
Cost modelling and capacity planning for AI workloads

modules.title: Multi-Agent Design and Orchestration
modules.description: Design and orchestrate multi-agent systems for complex workflows
topics: Agent design patterns: supervisor, collaborative, chain
Agent communication and handoff protocols
Tool use and function calling in multi-agent systems
Error handling and fallback strategies for agents
Testing and validating agent behaviour

modules.title: Microsoft AI Platform Integration
modules.description: Build solutions with Microsoft Copilot, Azure AI Foundry, and Dynamics 365
topics: Extending Microsoft 365 Copilot with custom agents
Azure AI Foundry for model deployment and management
Dynamics 365 AI integration patterns
Microsoft Graph as an AI data source
Power Platform Copilot Studio for low-code agents

modules.title: Production Governance and Operations
modules.description: Run agentic AI systems reliably in production
topics: Agent lifecycle management and versioning
Monitoring, observability, and alerting for AI agents
Compliance and audit trails for autonomous decisions
Incident response for AI system failures
Scaling and performance optimisation

handsOnLabs.title: AI Architecture Design Session
handsOnLabs.description: Design an AI-first architecture for a realistic enterprise scenario with multiple agent types

handsOnLabs.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration Lab
handsOnLabs.description: Build and test a multi-agent workflow using Copilot Studio and Azure AI Foundry

handsOnLabs.title: Agent Governance Implementation
handsOnLabs.description: Set up monitoring, compliance policies, and lifecycle management for AI agents

prerequisites: Familiarity with Azure AI services and Microsoft 365
Understanding of AI and machine learning concepts
targetAudience: Senior engineers leading AI platform teams
Technical leads responsible for AI strategy execution

instructor.name: Yair Knijn
instructor.title: Principal Cloud Architect & Engineer
instructor.bio: Azure Solutions Architect Expert and Microsoft Certified Trainer who designs agentic AI solutions for enterprise clients, from architecture through production deployment.

certification.name: Microsoft Certified: Agentic AI Business Solutions Architect

personalStory.story: Multi-agent systems are where AI gets real in the enterprise. I have built these architectures for clients who need autonomous agents to handle processes that are too complex for a single Copilot prompt. This course takes the architecture patterns I use in production and teaches you how to design, build, and govern them.
